title: MIDDAG Shared Props Contract
description: TypeScript contract for Inertia shared props — data sent on every request from the PHP backend. Read via usePage<SharedProps>().props. Consumed by providers (AuthProvider, FlashProvider, ScopeProvider, I18nProvider).

SharedPropsAuth.description: Authenticated user data from Inertia shared props. Consumed by AuthProvider → useAuth() hook.

avatarUrl.description: URL to user avatar (Moodle profile picture).

capabilities.description: Moodle capability strings the user holds in current context. Used by <Can>/<Cannot> gates.

logoutUrl.description: Host-signed logout URL (e.g. Moodle sesskey). Canonical location for the logout endpoint — the shell POSTs here to sign out. Present when the host injects it.

SharedPropsTheme.description: Theme and i18n data from Inertia shared props. Consumed by I18nProvider and theme system.

strings.description: Pre-loaded i18n strings for the current page (key → translated string).

appearance.description: Server-side appearance preference for cross-device sync.

brandColor.description: Moodle brand color (hex). Present when local_middag/inherit_theme_colors = ON.

inherit.description: Whether Moodle theme color inheritance is active.

SharedPropsFlash.description: Flash messages from backend. Consumed by FlashProvider → Sonner toasts.

AdminTabItem.description: A single tab in the admin tab bar rendered by ProductShell.

AdminTabsProps.description: Admin tab bar data. When present in shared props, ProductShell renders a tab bar between header and content.

active.description: Key of the currently active tab.

SharedProps.description: Full Inertia shared props shape. This is the top-level object available via usePage<SharedProps>().props on every page.

navigation.description: Sidebar navigation tree. See navigation.json schema.

auth.description: Authenticated user. Null or absent on guest-facing pages (e.g. login); AuthProvider tolerates its absence (user null, isAuthenticated false, every capability check false).

scope.description: Global scope data from global_scope_manager. Extensions add their keys via scope::set().

x-ai-purpose: These types describe the global data available on every page via Inertia shared props. They are NOT part of the PageContract — they are parallel data consumed by providers (AuthProvider, FlashProvider, ScopeProvider, I18nProvider). AI agents can assume navigation, theme, locale and version are always present; `auth` is optional and may be null on guest-facing pages (e.g. login) — never assume an authenticated user exists.
